sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. Hebr. צָבָא to go forth to battle, to wage war, Isa 29:7, צֹבֶיהָ i.q. צֹבְאִים עָלֶיהָ “those who make war with her.”

(2) to come forth, as a star, i.q. Arab. صبأ to come forth, to go out; hence to shine, to be bright, whence צְבִי splendour, glory.

(3) to project, to be prominent (Arab. صبا Conj. I. and ضبا Conj. II. to impend over any thing); hence to swell (used of the belly), Num 5:27, and, figuratively-

(4) to be inclined, to will, compare תָפֵץ and בָּעָה. (Arab. صبا id., Ch. and Syr. צְבָא, ܨܒܐܳ, which see.)

Hiphil, causat. of No. 3, to cause to swell, Num 5:22. Hence-
